channel.title: Craft
channel.description: Craft is a list of hand oriented craft and technology Do we really need all those power tools?

item.title: ShopNotes Podcast Episode 270 — It’s All About the Churn
item.description:
The bulk of this week’s episode addresses Logan’s shop changes — both recent and over the life of the construction and launch of this space. It’s been a lot of...

item.title: New Model Mandolin: 4
item.description:
The body of the mandolin is now together, so what’s next? In this video, I look at routing the rebate for the purflings and bindings. The purflings take quite a while to fit as there are tricky mitres to cut and the bindings (made from walnut) need to be bent on the bending iron to fit exactly. All good fun! Oh, and a bit about the fretboard too!

Cheers Gary
item.source:markdown:
The body of the mandolin is now together, so what’s next? In this video, I look at routing the rebate for the purflings and bindings. The purflings take quite a while to fit as there are tricky mitres to cut and the bindings (made from walnut) need to be bent on the bending iron to fit exactly. All good fun! Oh, and a bit about the fretboard too!
Cheers Gary
item.author: noreply@blogger.com (Gary Nava, Luthier)

item.title: Big Woodworking Fun with ‘Smalls’
item.description: Our new pocketbook, “Smalls: 18 Well-designed, Easy Projects for an Afternoon in the Workshop,” is in the hands of Kara Gebhart Uhl for a copy edit. I’ll get it back from her next week, make the final changes, then upload it to the printer. After that, it’s usually about six weeks until it’s in the...
item.source:markdown: Our new pocketbook, “Smalls: 18 Well-designed, Easy Projects for an Afternoon in the Workshop,” is in the hands of Kara Gebhart Uhl for a copy edit. item.title: 113. Sean McGowan
item.description:
For episode 113 of Luthier on Luthier, I'm joined by guitarist and educator Sean McGowan. 
 
We focus on his recent Archtop Foundation recording project, featuring 20 tracks across nine exceptional Blue series archtop guitars, and dig into his detailed approach to capturing their unique voices in the studio. Sean also shares highlights from his musical journey, his work as an educator at the University of Colorado in Denver, and the realities of making a living as a modern musician. 
 
Link: https://seanmcgowanguitar.com/
 
Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of Bashkin Guitars and brought to you by the Fretboard Journal. This episode is sponsored by the Looth Group, Dream Guitars and StewMac.
item.source:markdown:
For episode 113 of Luthier on Luthier, I'm joined by guitarist and educator Sean McGowan.
We focus on his recent Archtop Foundation recording project, featuring 20 tracks across nine exceptional Blue series archtop guitars, and dig into his detailed approach to capturing their unique voices in the studio. Sean also shares highlights from his musical journey, his work as an educator at the University of Colorado in Denver, and the realities of making a living as a modern musician.
Link: [https://seanmcgowanguitar.com/](https://seanmcgowanguitar.com/)
Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of [Bashkin Guitars](https://www.bashkinguitars.com) and brought to you by the [Fretboard Journal](https://shop.fretboardjournal.com/products/fretboard-journal-annual-subscription). This episode is sponsored by [the Looth Group](https://loothgroup.com), [Dream Guitars](https://www.dreamguitars.com/) and [StewMac](https://www.stewmac.com/?irclickid=VA-TmuXZ%3AxyPUn0Ut-05ZTupUkHUPAzGE2bmy00&utm_source=3755630&utm_medium=Impact&utm_campaign=3755630&utm_content=Online%20Tracking%20Link_1303370&irgwc=1&partner=Fretboard%20Journal&mpid=3755630&group=).

item.title: Revive or Restore a Finish?
item.description:
Discover how (and when) to give old, deteriorated finishes new life. As finishes age, they deteriorate. First they dull, then they begin showing small cracks (called “crazing”). The culprit of...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2020/04/16131246/01pwm1308flexner-1024x473.jpg)Discover how (and when) to give old, deteriorated finishes new life. As finishes age, they deteriorate. First they dull, then they begin showing small cracks (called “crazing”). The culprit of...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/finishing/flexner-on-finsihing-revive-or-restore/)

item.title: Mind Upon Mind: Stick Stop
item.description: Editor’s note: Our Mind Upon Mind series is a nod to a 1937 Chips from the Chisel column (also featured in “Honest Labour: The Charles H. Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...
item.source:markdown: Editor’s note: Our Mind Upon Mind series is a nod to a 1937 Chips from the Chisel column (also featured in “Honest Labour: The Charles H. Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...

item.title: Devilishly Clever ‘Doe’s Foot’
item.description:
These notched sticks simplify your workholding at the bench. Workbenches didn’t always have vises. In fact, for most of human history, workbenches around the world were nothing more than a...

item.title: LAP Open Wire, April 18, 2026
item.description: Comments are now closed – thanks to everyone who joined us today, and those reading later. Join us again on June 20! Got a woodworking question, a query about dog holes (which Kale is drilling above, for their new tail vise) or are curious to know what’s on the horizon at Lost Art Press? Today...

item.title: Slow Cooker
item.description: I’ve been at home this week (and possibly will be next) staring on and off at my not-yet-finished kitchen – on which I cannot currently work – every time I go to refresh my ice pack and drink more fluids. (I’m recovering from minor but uncomfortable surgery, and am under doctor’s orders to do no...

item.title: A Back-saving Vise Extension
item.description:
While sawing dovetails for a jewelry box, I found my back was hurting from hunching over my bench vise. In order to work more comfortably, I built this vise extension...

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/tricks/a-back-saving-vise-extension/)

item.title: Applications Open for Chairmaker’s Toolbox Class
item.description: The Chairmaker’s Toolbox is accepting applications for a scholarship class this August at Lost Art Press. All six students will receive free tuition and materials (plus some meals) during the week-long chairmaking class, Aug. 3-7. Apply for this scholarship class here. Applications close on May 15. The class will be taught by Grace Ambrose and...
